title: How North Korea Won: The Strange Triumph of Kim Jong Un
content:
The 75th anniversary of the Korean Workers Party in October 2020 was not the festive affair that North Korean leader Kim Jong Un wanted it to be. The Covid-19 pandemic had been tough for most countries, but it seemed especially portentous for North Korea. Kim himself was humiliated and isolated after failing to deliver much-needed sanctions relief in high-profile summitry. It was arguably the lowest moment in the country's modern history. Yet, just five years later, Kim was beaming at a military parade in Beijing, standing alongside global leaders.

North Korean soldiers were fighting alongside Russian troops, and trade with China had reached healthy pre-pandemic levels. Kim had been welcomed into a unified cohort of leaders countering Western influence. In a stunning reversal of fortune, North Korea is now ascendant in ways not even the most imaginative analyst could have predicted. Kim has transformed himself from a global pariah into a global power player in record time. This metamorphosis is the product of both circumstance and remarkable diplomatic skill.

The dawn of a new era of great-power competition has been an unwelcome development for many small countries. However, North Korea has fared better than most by leveraging its nuclear arsenal to avoid getting trampled by bigger players. Kim has proved uniquely adept at exploiting the opportunities and navigating the currents of this new geopolitics. By taking surprising risks with China and Russia, his ability to shape regional dynamics is now greater than ever. Today, the North Korea challenge is both more formidable and more durable.
